mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.5% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 2.5% and remains there for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 1.91% suggests easing environment
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during easing regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate above 4.2% signals labor market deterioration that increases recession risk, causing equity investors to reduce risk exposure and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as growth expectations contract.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.2% and remains elevated for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 3.9% is below trigger threshold
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite elevated unemployment, or correlation between UNRATE changes and forward SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth measured by RSAFS combined with housing starts measured by HOUST both declining simultaneously indicates weakening consumer demand and housing investment, signaling reduced aggregate demand that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth falls below 0.2% AND HOUST declines more than 5% month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 509025M and HOUST at 1168K units
failure_condition: Joint occurrence of both trigger conditions produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction effect (measured as residual correlation after removing individual series effects) is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index INDPRO declining below 107.5 signals supply-side contraction and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, indicating economic weakness that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 107.5 and remains below this level for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 108.2317 is above trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite industrial production decline, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment measured by PAYEMS combined with rising inflation measured by CPIAUCSL both accelerating simultaneously indicates tight labor markets driving wage-price spiral, causing the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth exceeds 0.3% AND CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4% in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 149279K and CPIAUCSL at 251.846
regime_hint: tight labor market with persistent inflation
failure_condition: Joint occurrence of both trigger conditions produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.12) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 accelerating above 2.5% annualized rate signals robust economic expansion that increases corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors increase equity allocations in response to growth acceleration.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate exceeds 2.5%
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of quarters with GDPC1 growth above 2.5%, or correlation between GDPC1 growth and forward SPX returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 3.2% combined with the federal funds rate remaining below 2.5% indicates real rates are negative, signaling monetary policy is accommodative and supporting equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth annualizes above 3.2% AND FEDFUNDS is simultaneously below 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 1.91% and CPIAUCSL at 251.846
regime_hint: negative real rates environment with accommodative policy
failure_condition: Joint occurrence of both trigger conditions produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the real rate signal (measured as FEDFUNDS minus annualized CPIAUCSL growth) shows no significant correlation with forward returns (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index INDPRO accelerating above 109.0 combined with payroll employment PAYEMS growing above 0.25% month-over-month indicates strong supply-side capacity expansion and labor productivity gains, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as profit margins expand.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above 109.0 AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth exceeds 0.25% in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 108.2317 and PAYEMS at 149279K
failure_condition: Joint occurrence of both trigger conditions produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction effect (measured as residual after removing individual series effects) is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in multivariate analysis.
